AgentEventKind.description: Payload of a [`RuntimeEvent::Agent`] — one step of the agent loop. Defined as transport-neutral data; the host maps its own agent events onto these.

cache_creation_tokens.description: Prompt tokens written into the provider's prompt cache, when reported.

cache_read_tokens.description: Prompt tokens served from the provider's prompt cache, when reported. Additive and optional: producers that don't track caching omit it.

ApprovalMode.description: Per-session approval posture controlling how high a [`RiskTier`] the gate may auto-approve without prompting. Pure protocol data; the host gate (P2) maps each tool's tier against [`Self::max_auto_tier`].

oneOf.description: Prompt for everything above read-only.
Auto-approve reads and edits; prompt for exec.
Auto-approve everything, including exec.

AuthEventKind.description: Authentication lifecycle event kind. Defined as pure protocol data; hosts map concrete credential/login implementation details onto these states.

BudgetSpec.description: The fleet budget envelope (design §6). C1 threads it as a placeholder; C3 wires the deterministic debit/cancel loop. All fields optional — an omitted budget caps nothing (the engine still bounds concurrency separately).

max_total_cost_usd.description: Total USD ceiling across the whole tree.

max_total_tokens.description: Total token ceiling across the whole tree.

max_workers.description: Maximum number of workers across the whole tree (the global semaphore).

ContextSplit.description: How a [`Strategy::MapReduce`] splits its shared context across map workers. Defined-ahead for C5; C1 never reads it.

oneOf.description: Split a build_context selection into `n` roughly equal shards.
One map worker per listed path group.

DelegateAutonomy.description: Autonomy posture handed to a delegated external agent CLI, mapping to each vendor's sandbox/permission flag: codex `--sandbox`, claude `--permission-mode`, gemini `--approval-mode` (read-only | edit | full). Defaults to the most restricted ([`Self::ReadOnly`]) so an omitted field never grants more than read access.

oneOf.description: The delegated agent may only read; no edits, no command execution.
The delegated agent may read and edit workspace files.
The delegated agent may read, edit, and run commands.

FailPolicy.description: What the engine does when a step's worker fails (`ok == false` or errored).

oneOf.description: Stop the whole flow on this failure (the safe default).
Record the failure and continue (used by `Parallel` branches).
Retry the step up to `n` more times before applying `Abort`.

FlowNodeUsage.description: A flow node's token usage, carried on [`RuntimeEvent::FlowNodeFinished`]. Mirrors the token fields of [`AgentEventKind::Usage`]; cache counts are optional and omitted when the worker did not report caching.

FlowRunOutcome.description: The aggregated outcome of a finished flow, carried on [`RuntimeEvent::FlowCompleted`]: whether the flow succeeded under its join/fail policy, a one-line summary, and the flow's final text (the kept results concatenated, in declared order).

FlowWorkerKind.description: Which worker family ran a flow node — the only place the CLI-vs-provider distinction is visible to a flow client (design §2/§7). Protocol data; the host maps its own worker kind onto these.

oneOf.description: An external agentic CLI (codex / claude / gemini) subprocess.
An in-process provider loop over the Nerve tool surface.

Join.description: How parallel/vote results are folded — always in **declared `Step` order**, never completion order (design §3, the determinism invariant).

oneOf.description: Keep every branch result (in declared order).
Keep the first OK result in declared order (a branch with `ok == true`).
Keep results once `n` branches succeed (the first `n` OKs in declared order); a short quorum yields whatever OKs there were.

RiskTier.description: Coarse risk classification for a tool, ordered least-to-most privileged. Advisory protocol data consumed by a future permission engine (P4); it does not gate execution today.

oneOf.description: Pure reads: catalog/search/navigation that never mutate state.
Mutates workspace files (writes, patches, moves, deletes).
Runs arbitrary commands or otherwise escapes the file sandbox.

RuntimeCommand.description: Transport-neutral command understood by human-facing runtime adapters.

oneOf.description: Lightweight health check used by clients before opening a real session.
Return all runtime tool specifications.
Execute one MCP-style tool through the runtime dispatcher.

Run the built-in agent loop as a job. This is protocol vocabulary only: the host job manager (the composition root) executes it; the core runtime dispatcher does not (it has no LLM/provider knowledge). Provider/model are plain data here and translated to domain types by the host.
Start or resume a host-managed interactive agent session.
Send a user message to an existing host-managed session.
Interrupt the current turn of an existing host-managed session.
Reply to a session approval request.
Fetch one host-managed session.
List host-managed sessions.
Close a host-managed session.
Switch the model (and optionally provider) of a live session in place, keeping its history and checkpoint. Takes effect from the next turn.
Switch the approval mode of a live session in place. Takes effect from the next gate decision. Pure protocol vocabulary; the host session manager stores it (P2 consults it in the gate).
Start a host-managed OAuth login and return an authorization URL.
Complete a host-managed OAuth login with a code or pasted callback URL.
Return stored OAuth/API-key credential status without secrets.
Remove stored credentials for a provider.

Delegate a coding task to an external agent CLI (codex / claude / gemini) as a long-lived job. Pure protocol vocabulary: the host job manager drives the subprocess (DA-2); `nerve-core` has no subprocess knowledge. `agent` is the catalog name from `list_agents`; `cwd` defaults to the workspace root; `model` overrides the agent's default model. Progress streams back as [`crate::RuntimeEvent::DelegateProgress`].

mcp_enable.description: DA-6 (codex only): the MCP allowlist for this delegated codex session — the `[mcp_servers.<name>]` entries to keep enabled; every other configured server is disabled for a fast start. `Some(list)` overrides the persisted `[delegate.codex] mcp_enable` config (an empty list disables ALL); `None` falls back to that config. Ignored for non-codex agents.

oneOf.description: Steer a live delegated session with a follow-up user message, running one more turn against the same long-lived agent process. Pure protocol vocabulary: the host job manager looks up the live session (DA-5a) and continues it; progress streams back as [`crate::RuntimeEvent::DelegateProgress`]. `session_id` is the `job_id` of the originating [`Self::DelegateStart`] job (a started delegated session keeps that id for its whole lifetime).

End a live delegated session: close the agent process's stdin (which it treats as EOF and exits on) and reap it. Pure protocol vocabulary; the host job manager deregisters the live session. `session_id` is the originating [`Self::DelegateStart`] job id (see [`Self::DelegateSteer`]).

anyOf.description: An inline workflow definition (the whole strategy as data). Boxed so the large inline-def variant doesn't bloat every command value (the named-ref variant is tiny).
A named workflow resolved from a loaded data file (loaded, not compiled).

oneOf.description: Start a declarative orchestration workflow (the Conductor, design §4) as one cancellable **job**: the host job manager runs the deterministic flow engine (C1) and the `job_id` IS the `flow_id`. The `workflow` is either an inline [`WorkflowDef`] or a named reference resolved from a loaded `WorkflowDef` data file ([`FlowSource`]). Progress streams back as the `flow_*` events ([`crate::RuntimeEvent::FlowStarted`] …); approvals reuse [`crate::RuntimeEvent::ApprovalRequested`] keyed by `flow_id`.

inputs.description: Named-output seeds the engine exposes to the first node's task template.

workspace.description: Workspace to run against when more than one is registered.

oneOf.description: Steer a live flow branch with a follow-up message, running one more turn against the same live worker session (design §4 / Wave C3a). Reuses the C0 [`WorkerSession::steer`] port per-worker, exactly as `delegate.steer` does for a single delegated session: the host job manager looks up the live worker for `target` in the live-flow worker registry and continues it; progress streams back as [`crate::RuntimeEvent::FlowNodeAgent`]. Only a live, steerable branch (a `Single`/`Pipeline` worker still in flight, on a steerable substrate) can be steered; a closed or one-shot worker (`gemini`) returns a clear error. `flow_id` is the originating [`Self::FlowStart`] job id; `target` selects which branch (by node id, or the only live worker when unset).
Fetch one live or recently-finished flow by id. Mirrors `session.get`.
List flows the host knows about. Mirrors `session.list`.
Close (cancel) a live flow, tearing its workers down. Mirrors `session.close` / `delegate.close`. `flow_id` is the originating [`Self::FlowStart`] job id.

Reply to a flow approval request. **Reuses** the existing [`SessionApprovalDecision`] + the host `ApprovalHub` round-trip, keyed by `flow_id` (a flow branch is just another approval id). No new approval type.

RuntimeEvent.description: Runtime event emitted by human-facing adapters while executing jobs.

oneOf.description: A structured step from the built-in agent loop, scoped to its job.
A host-managed session has been created or resumed.
A host-managed session has started processing a user turn.
A host-managed session is ready for the next client action.
A host-managed session has been closed.
A structured agent-loop step scoped to an interactive session.

Advisory streaming fragment of an in-progress tool call, scoped to its job. Carries a raw provider delta string; UI-only and additive — clients that don't render streaming tool calls may ignore it. The producer is wired in a later wave; this variant only reserves the protocol shape.
A session turn needs a client/human decision before continuing.

preview.description: Human-readable preview of what the call would do (e.g. a diff or command line). Additive; defaults to empty when not computed.

tier.description: Risk classification of the tool whose call is awaiting a decision. Additive; older emitters/clients that omit it default to the most-restricted tier ([`RiskTier::Exec`]).

oneOf.description: A host-managed authentication lifecycle update.

Streaming output fragment from a delegated external agent CLI, scoped to its job. `agent` is the catalog name (codex / claude / gemini); `text` is a raw stdout/stderr chunk. Additive and job-scoped; the producer is wired in DA-2 (this variant only reserves the protocol shape).

A flow run (the Conductor, design §4) has started. Carries the declarative [`Strategy`] so a client can render the DAG shape before any node runs. All `flow_*` events carry the `flow_id` (which is the flow job's id).
A flow node's worker has started. `worker` is a human-readable label (the CLI catalog name or `provider/model`); `kind` is the worker family (`cli` | `provider`) so a client can badge the node pane.
A flow node's worker has finished. `ok` is the node's success; `usage` is the node's token usage (zeroed when the worker reported none).
A DAG edge `from → to` between two flow nodes, for rendering the graph. Emitted as the engine wires a downstream node to its upstream producer.

A structured agent-loop step scoped to a flow node — **reuses [`AgentEventKind`] verbatim**, symmetric with [`Self::SessionAgent`], so the TUI renders a node pane exactly as a session pane keyed by `node_id`.
A flow finished with an aggregated outcome (the fold of the recorded node results, in declared order — design §3).
A flow failed. `node_id` names the offending node when the failure is node-local; `error` is a human-readable message.

RuntimeEventNotification.description: Params payload of a `runtime/event` notification: the event itself plus a monotonic, per-stream sequence number so clients can detect dropped events and request replay. The event fields are flattened, so this is backward compatible with clients that read the bare event from `params`; `event_seq` is an additive sibling field (defaulting to 0 until a later wave assigns the real monotonic value at emit time).

oneOf.description: A structured step from the built-in agent loop, scoped to its job.
A host-managed session has been created or resumed.
A host-managed session has started processing a user turn.
A host-managed session is ready for the next client action.
A host-managed session has been closed.
A structured agent-loop step scoped to an interactive session.

Advisory streaming fragment of an in-progress tool call, scoped to its job. Carries a raw provider delta string; UI-only and additive — clients that don't render streaming tool calls may ignore it. The producer is wired in a later wave; this variant only reserves the protocol shape.
A session turn needs a client/human decision before continuing.

preview.description: Human-readable preview of what the call would do (e.g. a diff or command line). Additive; defaults to empty when not computed.

tier.description: Risk classification of the tool whose call is awaiting a decision. Additive; older emitters/clients that omit it default to the most-restricted tier ([`RiskTier::Exec`]).

oneOf.description: A host-managed authentication lifecycle update.

Streaming output fragment from a delegated external agent CLI, scoped to its job. `agent` is the catalog name (codex / claude / gemini); `text` is a raw stdout/stderr chunk. Additive and job-scoped; the producer is wired in DA-2 (this variant only reserves the protocol shape).

A flow run (the Conductor, design §4) has started. Carries the declarative [`Strategy`] so a client can render the DAG shape before any node runs. All `flow_*` events carry the `flow_id` (which is the flow job's id).
A flow node's worker has started. `worker` is a human-readable label (the CLI catalog name or `provider/model`); `kind` is the worker family (`cli` | `provider`) so a client can badge the node pane.
A flow node's worker has finished. `ok` is the node's success; `usage` is the node's token usage (zeroed when the worker reported none).
A DAG edge `from → to` between two flow nodes, for rendering the graph. Emitted as the engine wires a downstream node to its upstream producer.

A structured agent-loop step scoped to a flow node — **reuses [`AgentEventKind`] verbatim**, symmetric with [`Self::SessionAgent`], so the TUI renders a node pane exactly as a session pane keyed by `node_id`.
A flow finished with an aggregated outcome (the fold of the recorded node results, in declared order — design §3).
A flow failed. `node_id` names the offending node when the failure is node-local; `error` is a human-readable message.

eventSeq.description: Monotonically increasing, gap-detectable sequence number for this stream.

RuntimeJobError.description: Structured error stored on failed runtime jobs.

RuntimeJobSnapshot.description: Snapshot of a runtime job owned by a transport/daemon implementation.

RuntimeJobStatus.description: Status values used by daemon-owned runtime jobs.

RuntimeToolSpec.description: Runtime-visible tool specification shape shared with frontend clients.

SessionApprovalDecision.description:
Decision supplied by a human/client for a session approval request.

`Allow`/`Deny` apply to this call only; `AllowAlways`/`DenyAlways` additionally signal the host to remember the decision for future calls (P2 wires the remembering; P1 only distinguishes allow-vs-deny via [`Self::allows`]).

oneOf.description: Allow this call only.
Deny this call only.
Allow this call and remember the allow for future matching calls.
Deny this call and remember the deny for future matching calls.

Step.description: One unit of orchestrated work: which worker, what task, how autonomous, and what to do if it fails.

autonomy.description: Autonomy posture (maps to the CLI sandbox flag / provider exec capability).

on_fail.description: What the engine does when this step's worker fails.

task.description: The task handed to the worker (with minimal named-output interpolation).

Strategy.description:
The closed, additive-versioned set of orchestration combinators (design §3).

C1 implements `Single` + `Parallel`; the rest are defined-ahead for C5 so the data is stable. `#[non_exhaustive]` keeps adding variants additive for downstream matchers, and the engine's interpreter matches every variant explicitly (returning `Terminate` with an "unimplemented strategy" outcome for the not-yet-wired ones), so the totality is compile-checked.

oneOf.description: Run one worker and return its result (design §3). **Implemented in C1.**
Fan out to N branches in parallel, then fold by `join` in **declared order** (design §3, the load-bearing invariant). **Implemented in C1.**
Run stages in sequence; stage N reads the ledger outputs of stages `< N`. Defined-ahead for C3/C5.
Map a step over a context split, then reduce. Defined-ahead for C5.
Generate `candidates`, keep the top `k`, and let a `judge` pick. Defined-ahead for C5.
Multi-round debate between `sides`, adjudicated by `judge`. Defined-ahead for C5.
A planner emits a child strategy the engine runs bounded by `max_depth` (design §8). Defined-ahead for C5.

TaskTemplate.description: A minimal task template (design §3, open question 3): a plain prompt plus optional `{{name}}` placeholders substituted from named ledger outputs. **No expression language** — named-output substitution only, deliberately.

prompt.description: The raw prompt with optional `{{name}}` placeholders.

WorkerRef.description: Which worker runs a [`Step`]. The only place the CLI-vs-provider distinction is encoded in the data (design §7); the engine resolves it through the `WorkerFactory` to a kind-agnostic `AgentWorker`.

oneOf.description: An external agentic CLI by catalog name (`codex` | `claude` | `gemini`).
An in-process provider loop by provider + model.
A worker resolved from a named `WorkerDef` data file (design §6, C6). Defined ahead; the C1 engine does not resolve `Named` yet.

WorkerSelector.description: Which live branch a [`RuntimeCommand::FlowSteer`] targets (design §4, the `WorkerSelector` row). Deliberately MINIMAL: a flow branch is identified by its deterministic node id, or — when `node_id` is unset — "the single live worker" (the common case for a `Single`/`Pipeline` flow, which has exactly one live branch at a time). An ambiguous unset selector against a multi-branch live flow is refused by the host with a clear message; the closed enum keeps the steer surface from drifting into a query language.

node_id.description: The deterministic node id of the branch to steer (e.g. `"node-0"` for a `Single` flow, `"stage-1"` for a pipeline's second stage). `None` targets the only live worker, erroring if more than one is live.

WorkflowDef.description: A complete, declarative workflow: a named [`Strategy`] plus the fleet-wide governance envelope (design §3). `schema_version` is bumped only on a breaking change to the data shape (additive fields don't bump it).

budget.description: Fleet budget envelope (design §6). Threaded as a placeholder in C1 — the debit/cancel loop lands in C3.

max_depth.description: Hierarchy depth ceiling (design §8). Default 2, matching today's `DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH`. Threaded as a placeholder in C1.

name.description: A human-readable name for the workflow (surfaced in logs / the ledger).

schema_version.description: The on-disk schema version of this workflow document (current: `1`).

strategy.description: The orchestration strategy the engine interprets.
